metric_value: Between one-third and half of ~1,000 companies surveyed having serious difficulties moving SOA-enabled applications into stable deployment
notes: Survey: ~1000 companies; conducted 2006; data in SOA Management and Governance Benchmark Report (February 2007).

metric_name: Root cause of SOA deployment difficulty
metric_value: Predominant reason: inadequate tools for automated management and governance of growing plethora and complexity of web services and applications; exacerbated by inexperience

metric_name: SOA development cost decrease — ALM practitioners
metric_value: 68% of ALM-focused organizations decreased SOA development costs over last 12 months
notes: Table 1: How Application Lifecycle Management Practitioners Led the Field. Source: AberdeenGroup February 2007.

metric_name: SOA development cost decrease — Best in Class
metric_value: 46% of Best in Class organizations decreased SOA development costs over last 12 months

metric_name: SOA development cost decrease — all respondents
metric_value: 25% of all respondents decreased SOA development costs over last 12 months

metric_name: No change in SOA costs — ALM practitioners
metric_value: 12% of ALM-focused organizations had no change in SOA development costs

metric_name: No change in SOA costs — Best in Class
metric_value: 44% of Best in Class had no change in SOA development costs
20% of ALM-focused organizations saw SOA development costs increase
35% of all respondents saw SOA development costs increase
Establishment of operational security, governance, and management: cited by 45% of survey base as top challenge
Security issues different from those of older IT: cited by 38% as challenge
Debugging problems with complex services and composites: cited by 37% as challenge
SLAs scaling to production volumes, reliability and availability: cited by 37% as challenge
Data management of SOA services is problematic: cited by 33% as challenge
QA testing and deployment stage problems spill over into production: cited by 31% as challenge
Revised application lifecycle processes and responsibilities: 45% chose as top response
notes: Table 2 responses column. Automotive parts manufacturer quote: 'With SOA, we were forced to rewrite whole chapters of our IT processes manual… and that's not a bad thing.' Source: AberdeenGroup February 2007.

metric_value: Engaged third-party IT services consulting, evaluation, and assessments: 41% response

metric_name: Purchase SOA operations management software intent
metric_value: Purchase or plan to purchase SOA operations management software: 28% response
Purchase or plan to purchase SOA security software: 26% response
Average investment: $2.68 million; average payback: $3.45 million; ROI: +29%
notes: Table 3: Average Investments and Paybacks in SOA Development. Source: AberdeenGroup February 2007.

metric_value: Average investment: $1.37 million; average payback: $1.18 million; ROI: -14%
notes: Table 3. All respondents have yet to see positive return from SOA investments on average. Source: AberdeenGroup February 2007.

metric_value: 12% of survey respondents are actively engaged in application lifecycle management for SOA
notes: Small but high-performing segment. All are part of Best in Class group.

metric_value: 48% of ALM-focused organizations have annual revenue >$1 billion — Global 1000 lead the pack
notes: Large organizations dominate ALM adoption. Complex distributed organizations drive need.

metric_value: 57% of ALM practitioners have had SOA services deployed for more than 1 year vs. 31% of overall survey
29% of ALM practitioners have >100 SOA services deployed vs. 11% of overall survey
52% of ALM practitioners have SOA governance for BOTH design and run-time operations vs. 29% of overall survey and 29% of Best in Class
notes: ALM practitioners significantly more comprehensive in governance coverage.

metric_name: SOA governance as IT governance update
metric_value: For 42% of ALM companies, SOA governance is an update to existing IT governance processes
50% of ALM companies want to ensure uniform and consistent policy application across the organization as driver for SOA governance
notes: Policy consistency cited as primary governance motivation.

metric_value: Lack of SOA management and governance lowers IT performance, raises current operating costs, and collars IT with long-term software maintenance costs that will endure for decades

2006 surveys showed companies following 'Enterprise SOA' strategy (ESB) can scale more easily with lower short- and long-term costs by investing in all aspects of SOA infrastructure software and IT staff training
notes: Cross-reference to 2006 survey findings. Reinforces Enterprise SOA > SOA Lite argument.

metric_name: Top SOA management and governance driver
metric_value: Developing new business capabilities or new products and services: top driver for SOA management and governance
notes: Business capability development more important than IT complexity management as driver.
